universe.eligibility_scope: All existing constituents of the Parent Index.

mandatory_fields.reason: Red Flag exclusion and quarterly screen
Unrated treatment; securities without calculable Emission Intensity are ineligible
Climate Risk Management Eligibility and scoring
Green Business Score and Security-Level Assessment adjustment
Emission Eligibility and Security-Level Assessment adjustment
All sector-relative quartile calculations and sector weight caps
Tie-breaking in sector rankings and weighting
Renormalization and active sector weight calculation

preprocessing.description: 95th percentile of Emission Intensity among MSCI ACWI constituents with emission data; computed from MSCI ACWI Index constituents.

95th percentile of Total Potential Carbon Emissions excluding Metallurgical Coal among MSCI ACWI constituents where fossil_fuel_reserves_energy_application is True and total potential carbon emissions data is present.

Weighted average of 7 MSCI ESG Ratings key issue management scores (Carbon Emissions, Product Carbon Footprint, Climate Change Vulnerability, Biodiversity and Land Use, Clean Tech Opportunities, Green Building Opportunities, Renewable Energy Opportunities). Missing key issue scores are substituted with zero. If weighted average is zero or cannot be calculated and carbon emissions key issue weight is zero, set equal to Carbon Emissions Management Score.

Geometric average of last 3 yearly YoY emission changes. Assigned only to securities where: Average Yearly Emission Change < -2%, security reports Scope 1 and 2 emissions, and security has published an emission reduction target. Securities with fewer than 4 years of emission data are not assigned a track record.

Cumulative % revenue from six Clean Tech themes: Alternative Energy, Energy Efficiency, Sustainable Water, Green Building, Pollution Prevention, Sustainable Agriculture (sourced from MSCI Sustainable Impact Metrics).

details.missing_products: Companies not assessed by MSCI Solutions on MSCI Controversies, MSCI Climate Change Metrics, or MSCI Business Involvement Screening Research are excluded from the index.
details.missing_emission_intensity: Companies for which Emission Intensity cannot be calculated are excluded.
details.crm_missing_key_issues: For Climate Risk Management Weighted Average Score calculation, missing key issue management scores are substituted with zero rather than excluding the security. See preprocessing step crm_weighted_avg_score_calculation.

new_rules.name: exclude_red_flag_controversies
new_rules.purpose: Exclude companies with an ongoing Very Severe controversy (Red Flag) directly implicating the company through its actions, products, or operations.

source.markdown: All companies assessed as having involvement in controversies that are classified as Red Flags (MSCI Controversies Score of 0).

technical: msci_controversies_score column present after filter
no null values in msci_controversies_score for remaining rows

new_rules.name: exclude_tobacco
new_rules.purpose: Exclude tobacco producers and companies deriving significant revenue from tobacco-related activities.

source.markdown: All companies classified as a 'Producer'. All companies deriving 5% or more aggregate revenue from the production, distribution, retail, supply, and licensing of tobacco related products.
source.section: Appendix I: ESG Business Involvement Eligibility

technical: tobacco_producer_flag and tobacco_revenue_pct columns present after filter
business: no remaining security has tobacco_producer_flag == True

new_rules.name: exclude_thermal_coal_mining
new_rules.purpose: Exclude companies with material revenue from thermal coal mining and external sale; excludes metallurgical coal, internal power generation, intra-company sales, and trading revenue.

source.markdown: All companies deriving 1% or more revenue (either reported or estimated) from the mining of thermal coal (including lignite, bituminous, anthracite and steam coal) and its sale to external parties.
source.section: Appendix I: ESG Business Involvement Eligibility

definition_notes: Excludes revenue from metallurgical coal
Excludes coal mined for internal power generation
Excludes intra-company sales of mined thermal coal
Excludes revenue from coal trading

technical: thermal_coal_mining_revenue_pct column present after filter

new_rules.name: exclude_oil_sands
new_rules.purpose: Exclude companies with material revenue from oil sands extraction that own reserves and disclose extraction revenue.

source.markdown: All companies deriving 5% or more revenue from oil sands extraction, which own oil sands reserves and disclose evidence of deriving revenue from oil sands extraction.
source.section: Appendix I: ESG Business Involvement Eligibility

definition_notes: Excludes revenue from non-extraction activities (exploration, surveying, processing, refining)
Excludes ownership of reserves with no associated extraction revenue
Excludes intra-company sales

technical: oil_sands_extraction_revenue_pct and has_oil_sands_reserves columns present after filter
business: no remaining security satisfies oil_sands_extraction_revenue_pct >= 5.0 AND has_oil_sands_reserves == True

new_rules.name: exclude_nuclear_weapons
new_rules.purpose: Exclude companies involved in manufacturing, assembling, or servicing nuclear warheads, missiles, dedicated components, delivery platforms, or auxiliary nuclear weapons services.

source.markdown: All companies that manufacture nuclear warheads and/or whole nuclear missiles... All companies that manufacture components that were developed or are significantly modified for exclusive use in nuclear weapons...
source.section: Appendix I: ESG Business Involvement Eligibility

definition_notes: Covers assembly and integration of warhead and missile body
Covers contracts to operate/manage government-owned facilities that manufacture nuclear warheads and missiles
Covers components developed or significantly modified for exclusive nuclear use
Covers delivery platforms developed or significantly modified for exclusive nuclear delivery
Covers auxiliary services: repair, maintenance, overhaul, upgrade, stockpiling, R&D, testing, simulations
Also covers components and platforms NOT exclusively developed for nuclear use but capable of nuclear application

technical: nuclear_weapons_flag column present after filter
business: no remaining security has nuclear_weapons_flag == True

new_rules.name: exclude_high_emitters_without_sbti
new_rules.purpose: Exclude high-emission companies without approved science-based targets to retain only those with either low emissions or a credible emissions commitment.

source.markdown: Securities which either have higher Emission Intensity than the High Emission Threshold... or higher total potential emission than the High Potential Emission Threshold... and do not yet have approved science-based targets are excluded.

definition_notes: High Emission Threshold: 95th percentile of Emission Intensity among MSCI ACWI constituents with emission data

High Potential Emission Threshold: 95th percentile of Total Potential Carbon Emissions ex Metallurgical Coal among MSCI ACWI constituents where fossil_fuel_reserves_energy_application is True and data is present
Exclusion applies when EITHER threshold is breached AND approved_sbti is False
Securities without Total Potential Carbon Emissions data are excluded from the 95th percentile calculation for that threshold

technical: emission_intensity and approved_sbti columns present after filter
business: no remaining security breaches either emission threshold without approved_sbti

new_rules.name: exclude_bottom_quartile_climate_risk_management
new_rules.purpose: Exclude securities with the least favorable Climate Risk Management assessment relative to sector peers (lowest quartile by sector-relative CRM Weighted Average Score).

source.markdown: All securities which are in the lowest quartile of their GICS sector based on their Climate Risk Management Score are excluded.

technical: crm_weighted_avg_score and gics_sector columns present after filter
business: no remaining security has sector-relative CRM quartile score of 1

new_rules.name: score_intensity
new_rules.purpose: Assign each security a sector-relative quartile (1-4) based on Scope 1+2+3 GHG Emission Intensity; score 4 indicates highest (worst) emission intensity within sector.

source.markdown: Intensity Score - Each security is assigned to a quartile based on its sector relative Scope 1+2+3 Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Intensity.

technical: intensity_score column added, dtype int, no nulls
business: within each gics_sector, intensity_score is distributed across all four quartiles

new_rules.name: score_green_business_revenue
new_rules.purpose: Assign each security a sector-relative quartile (1-4) based on cumulative Green Business Revenue from six Clean Tech themes; score 4 indicates highest green revenue within sector.

source.markdown: Green Business Revenue - Security level cumulative % of revenue from a set of 'Clean Tech' themes described in Appendix IV.
source.section: Appendix II: Calculation of Security Level Scores

technical: green_business_score column added, dtype int, no nulls

new_rules.name: score_climate_risk_management
new_rules.purpose: Assign each security a sector-relative quartile (1-4) based on CRM Weighted Average Score; score 4 indicates most favorable CRM assessment within sector.

source.markdown: Climate Risk Management Raw Score - Security level weighted average of a set of climate change related key issue management scores which are described in Appendix III.
source.section: Appendix II: Calculation of Security Level Scores

technical: climate_risk_management_score column added, dtype int, no nulls

new_rules.name: score_emission_track_record
new_rules.purpose: Assign each security a sector-relative quartile (1-4) based on Emission Track Record (geometric avg YoY change); score 1 indicates strongest historic emission reductions within sector.

source.markdown: Emission Track Record – Security level track record is calculated as follows: Security level yearly (YoY) change in emission is calculated for last 4 years (3 yearly changes).
source.section: Appendix II: Calculation of Security Level Scores

definition_notes: Only securities with emission_track_record assigned (meeting all three conditions) participate in quartile ranking
Securities without a track record value do not receive a track record score
Score 4 corresponds to weakest track record (least negative or positive change); score 1 corresponds to strongest reduction

technical: track_record_score column added; null is permissible for securities without emission_track_record

new_rules.name: assess_credible_track_record
new_rules.purpose: Flag a security as having Credible Track Record if and only if its Track Record Score is 1.

source.markdown: Security is assessed to have Credible Track Record if it is assigned a Track Record Score of 1.
source.section: Appendix II: Calculation of Security Level Scores

technical: credible_track_record column added, dtype bool, null permissible where track_record_score is null
business: credible_track_record is True only where track_record_score == 1

new_rules.name: compute_security_level_assessment
new_rules.purpose: Derive the final Security-Level Assessment by adjusting the Intensity Score downward for companies with approved SBTi or credible track record, then for companies with strong CRM or Green Business scores, floored at 1.

source.markdown: Each security is assigned a final Assessment by adjusting the initial Intensity Score according to the following sequence: The Intensity Score is lowered by two quartiles for all securities that either have an approved science-based targets (SBTi) or are assessed to have Credible Track Record.

definition_notes: Tier 1 (SBTi or Credible Track Record): lower intensity_score by 2; Tier 1 takes precedence, Tier 2 not applied
Tier 2 (neither SBTi nor Credible Track Record): lower intensity_score by 1 if climate_risk_management_score == 4 OR (green_business_score == 4 AND green_business_revenue_pct >= 5.0)
Assessment floored at 1 regardless of adjustment magnitude

technical: security_level_assessment column added, dtype int, no nulls
business: any security with approved_sbti or credible_track_record has security_level_assessment <= max(1, intensity_score - 2)

new_rules.name: rank_by_assessment_then_mcap
new_rules.purpose: Construct the Ranked Universe by ordering eligible securities within each GICS sector ascending by Security-Level Assessment and descending by float-adjusted market cap to break ties.

source.markdown: The Ranked Universe is constructed by sorting eligible securities of each GICS sector, in ascending order of Security-Level Assessment and descending order of float adjusted market capitalization.

technical: sector_rank column added per gics_sector, no nulls, integer starting at 1
business: within each gics_sector, rank is strictly ordered by ascending security_level_assessment then descending ff_mcap

new_rules.name: select_top_50pct_with_buffer
new_rules.purpose: Select approximately 50% of Applicable Universe securities per GICS sector using a buffer band that preferentially retains existing constituents ranked between 40% and 60%.

source.markdown: For each GICS sector in the Ranked Universe, 50% of the number of securities in the Applicable Universe are selected in the following order...

definition_notes: Step 1: Select all securities ranked above the 40% cutoff (rank <= floor(0.40 * N_applicable_sector))
Step 2: From remaining securities ranked in the 40-60% band, select existing constituents first
Step 3: Continue selecting non-constituent securities in the band until total selected <= 50% of N; any single security that would take total beyond 50% is also selected

technical: selected flag added as boolean column, no nulls
business: selected count per sector is approximately 50% of applicable universe count per sector
existing constituents in the 40-60% band are selected before non-constituents

new_rules.name: renormalize_parent_weights
new_rules.purpose: Set each selected security's weight to its Parent Index weight renormalized so that selected securities sum to 100%.

source.markdown: The Parent Index weights of selected securities are renormalized to 100%.

technical: weight column modified in-place, no nulls, all non-negative

new_rules.name: cap_issuer_weight_5pct
new_rules.purpose: Cap any single issuer's weight at 5% to limit concentration, redistributing excess weight across remaining securities.

source.markdown: Additionally, the max issuer weights are capped at 5%.

technical: weight column non-negative after cap, no nulls

new_rules.name: apply_active_sector_weight_cap_floor
new_rules.purpose: Restrict each GICS sector's active weight (index weight minus Parent Index weight) to a +/-5% band, then redistribute remaining sector weights to restore 100% total.

source.markdown: Active sector weights are capped in the following steps: Active sector weights are calculated by aggregating the active weight of securities in the Parent Index... Active sector weights which are greater than +5% are capped at +5% and active sector weights which are smaller than -5% are floored at -5%.
source.section: 2.4.1 Active GICS Sector Weight Cap and Floor

definition_notes: Active weight per security = weight (from section 2.4) - parent_index_weight
Active sector weight = sum of active weights for all securities in the sector
After capping/flooring sector active weights, remaining sectors are renormalized to sum all sectors to 100%
Within each capped/floored sector, the sector weight is distributed to selected securities in proportion to their section-2.4 weights

technical: weight column non-negative after redistribution, no nulls

new_rules.name: spinoff_immediate_addition
new_rules.purpose: Add all securities created from the spin-off of an existing index constituent at event implementation time; defer eligibility re-evaluation to the next scheduled Index Review.

source.markdown: All securities created as a result of the spin-off of an existing Index constituent will be added to the Index at the time of event implementation. Reevaluation for continued inclusion in the Index will occur at the subsequent Index Review.

business: all spin-off securities of existing constituents are present in the index at event implementation date

new_rules.name: ma_acquirer_proportionate_weight
new_rules.purpose: On merger or acquisition, adjust the acquirer's post-event weight to reflect the proportionate amount of shares involved in deal consideration; invest cash proceeds across the index.

source.markdown: For Mergers and Acquisitions, the acquirer's post event weight will account for the proportionate amount of shares involved in deal consideration, while cash proceeds will be invested across the Index.

business: acquirer weight post-event reflects deal consideration proportion
cash proceeds reinvested pro-rata across remaining constituents

new_rules.name: quarterly_controversies_bisr_deletion
new_rules.purpose: At each quarterly review, screen existing constituents for Controversies Score Eligibility and ESG Business Involvement Eligibility; delete constituents that fail and renormalize remaining weights to 100%.

source.markdown: During quarterly reviews, the 'Controversies Score Eligibility' and 'ESG Business Involvement Eligibility' of existing constituents are reviewed. Existing constituents that meet the exclusion criteria are deleted from the index and the weight of remaining constituents are renormalized to 100%.

technical: weight column sums to 1.0 after renormalization
business: no remaining constituent fails any ESG business involvement exclusion criterion

reused_rules.name: exclude_controversial_weapons_involvement
reused_rules.justification: Appendix I refers to 'the methodology of the MSCI Global Ex-Controversial Weapons Indexes' (URL cited). The XCW rule implements the identical BISR-based controversial weapons flag covering cluster munitions, landmines, depleted uranium, biological/chemical, blinding lasers, non-detectable fragments, and incendiary weapons.

reused_rules.name: xcw_new_addition_deferred_to_review
reused_rules.justification: Source text 'A new security added to the Parent Index (such as IPO and other early inclusions) will not be added to the index' is transformationally identical to the XCW rule that filters is_new_parent_addition == True; defer new parent additions to the next Index Review.

reused_rules.name: xcw_parent_deletion_propagates
reused_rules.justification: Source text 'Parent Index deletions will be reflected simultaneously' is transformationally identical to the XCW rule that removes securities flagged is_parent_deletion_event.

reused_rules.name: xcw_ma_delete_acquired_constituent
reused_rules.justification: Source text 'If an existing Index constituent is acquired by a non-Index constituent, the existing constituent will be deleted from the Index' is transformationally identical to XCW's delete-acquired-constituent rule.

reused_rules.name: xcw_ma_no_add_nonconstituent_acquirer
reused_rules.justification: Source text '...and the acquiring non-constituent will not be added to the Index' is the separable companion transformation to xcw_ma_delete_acquired_constituent and is transformationally identical to the XCW no-add-acquirer rule.

reused_rules.name: xcw_characteristics_change_no_deletion
reused_rules.justification: Source text 'A security will continue to be an Index constituent if there are changes in characteristics (country, sector, size segment, etc.) Reevaluation for continued inclusion in the Index will occur at the subsequent Index Review' is transformationally identical to the XCW characteristics-change no-deletion rule.

variables.name: intensity_score
variables.scale: 1-4 sector-relative quartile; 4 = highest emission intensity in sector

variables.name: green_business_score
variables.scale: 1-4 sector-relative quartile; 4 = highest green revenue in sector

variables.name: climate_risk_management_score
variables.scale: 1-4 sector-relative quartile; 4 = most favorable CRM in sector

variables.name: track_record_score
variables.scale: 1-4 sector-relative quartile; 1 = strongest historical emission reduction; null for securities without a track record

variables.name: security_level_assessment
variables.ranking_metric: derived from intensity_score via sequential adjustment

conditional_adjustments.adjustment: lower intensity_score by 2 quartiles, floor at 1; Tier 2 not applied

conditional_adjustments.condition: Tier 1 not triggered AND (climate_risk_management_score == 4 OR (green_business_score == 4 AND green_business_revenue_pct >= 5.0))
conditional_adjustments.adjustment: lower intensity_score by 1 quartile, floor at 1

content.incumbent_preference: existing constituents in the 40-60% band are selected before non-constituents in the same band
content.overshoot_policy: any security that would take the total selected beyond 50% is still included
content.scope: applied per GICS sector using Applicable Universe count as denominator

caps.description: No issuer may exceed 5% of index weight
No GICS sector active weight may exceed +5% relative to Parent Index

floors.description: No GICS sector active weight may fall below -5% relative to Parent Index

schedules.name: Semi-Annual Index Review
schedules.announcement_lead: nine business days before effective date
schedules.data_cutoff: end of month preceding the Index Review

schedules.name: Quarterly Index Review
schedules.announcement_lead: nine business days before effective date
schedules.data_cutoff: end of month preceding the Index Review (MSCI Controversies and BISR data)

defers_to.section: Capped Weighted and Non-Market Capitalization Weighted indexes

intra_review_invariants.quote: No new securities will be added (except where noted below) to the Index between Index Reviews.
intra_review_invariants.concern: Applies between semi-annual reviews; spin-offs of existing constituents are the sole exception where additions occur at event time.

intra_review_invariants.quote: Parent Index deletions will be reflected simultaneously.
intra_review_invariants.concern: Parent Index deletions propagate to this index at event time, contrary to the general no-change-between-reviews stance.

intra_review_invariants.quote: A security will continue to be an Index constituent if there are changes in characteristics (country, sector, size segment, etc.)
intra_review_invariants.concern: Characteristics changes (country reclassification, sector reclassification, size segment change) do not trigger deletion between reviews; re-evaluation deferred to next review.

final_asserts: active_sector_weight.abs().max() <= 0.05 + 1e-6: all sector active weights within +/-5% band
msci_controversies_score.min() > 0: no Red Flag security included
nuclear_weapons_flag.any() == False: no nuclear weapons involvement
security_level_assessment.between(1, 4).all(): all assessments in valid range
per-sector selected count is approximately 50% of applicable universe count per sector

mock_datapoints.name: msci_controversies_score
mock_datapoints.description: MSCI Controversies Score on 0-10 scale; 0 indicates Red Flag (ongoing Very Severe controversy).
mock_datapoints.source_section: 2.2.1 Controversies Score Eligibility
mock_datapoints.taxonomy: MSCI Controversies 0-10 scale; 0=Red Flag (most severe), 10=least severe
definition_notes: Red Flag indicates an ongoing, Very Severe controversy implicating a company directly through its actions, products, or operations

mock_datapoints.name: tobacco_producer_flag
mock_datapoints.description: True if company is classified as a tobacco Producer by MSCI BISR.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix I: ESG Business Involvement Eligibility

mock_datapoints.name: tobacco_revenue_pct
mock_datapoints.description: Aggregate revenue percentage from production, distribution, retail, supply, and licensing of tobacco-related products.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix I: ESG Business Involvement Eligibility
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: 5% or more aggregate revenue from the production, distribution, retail, supply, and licensing of tobacco related products

mock_datapoints.name: thermal_coal_mining_revenue_pct
mock_datapoints.description: Revenue percentage (reported or estimated) from mining thermal coal (lignite, bituminous, anthracite, steam coal) and sale to external parties.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix I: ESG Business Involvement Eligibility
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: 1% or more revenue (either reported or estimated) from the mining of thermal coal ... and its sale to external parties
definition_notes: Excludes metallurgical coal
Excludes coal mined for internal power generation
Excludes intra-company sales
Excludes coal trading revenue

mock_datapoints.name: oil_sands_extraction_revenue_pct
mock_datapoints.description: Revenue percentage from oil sands extraction by companies that own oil sands reserves and disclose extraction revenue.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix I: ESG Business Involvement Eligibility
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: 5% or more revenue from oil sands extraction, which own oil sands reserves and disclose evidence of deriving revenue from oil sands extraction
definition_notes: Excludes non-extraction activities (exploration, surveying, processing, refining)
Excludes reserve ownership with no extraction revenue
Excludes intra-company sales

mock_datapoints.name: has_oil_sands_reserves
mock_datapoints.description: True if company owns oil sands reserves.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix I: ESG Business Involvement Eligibility

mock_datapoints.name: nuclear_weapons_flag
mock_datapoints.description: True if company is involved in manufacturing, assembling, or servicing nuclear warheads, missiles, dedicated components, delivery platforms, or auxiliary nuclear weapons services as defined by MSCI BISR.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix I: ESG Business Involvement Eligibility
definition_notes: Includes components not exclusively designed for nuclear use but capable of nuclear application
Includes delivery platforms not exclusively designed for nuclear delivery but capable of it
Includes contracts to operate government-owned nuclear facilities

mock_datapoints.name: emission_intensity
mock_datapoints.description: Global GHG Emission (Scope 1+2+3, CO2-equivalent) divided by (Enterprise Value + Cash in USD).
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix II: Calculation of Security Level Scores
definition_notes: GHG includes CO2, HFCs, CH4, N2O, PFCs, SF6 per GHG Protocol
Enterprise Value + Cash is the denominator

mock_datapoints.name: has_emission_intensity_data
mock_datapoints.description: True if Emission Intensity can be calculated for the security.
mock_datapoints.source_section: 2.5 Treatment Of Unrated Companies

mock_datapoints.name: total_potential_carbon_emissions_ex_met_coal
mock_datapoints.description: Potential carbon emissions from fossil fuel reserves excluding metallurgical coal; sum of potential carbon emissions from thermal coal, total oil, and total gas reserves owned by the company.
mock_datapoints.source_section: 2.2.3 Emission Eligibility
definition_notes: Applies only to securities with total potential carbon emissions data available

mock_datapoints.name: fossil_fuel_reserves_energy_application
mock_datapoints.description: True if company's fossil fuel reserves are most likely to be used for energy applications (MSCI 'Fossil Fuel Reserves – Energy Application' = True).
mock_datapoints.source_section: 2.2.3 Emission Eligibility
definition_notes: Used to filter the population for the 95th percentile High Potential Emission Threshold calculation

mock_datapoints.name: approved_sbti
mock_datapoints.description: True if company has company-level emission reduction targets approved by the Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi).
mock_datapoints.source_section: 2.2.3 Emission Eligibility

mock_datapoints.name: crm_weighted_avg_score
mock_datapoints.description: Climate Risk Management Weighted Average Score: weighted average of Carbon Emissions Management, Product Carbon Footprint Management, Climate Change Vulnerability Management, Biodiversity and Land Use Management, Clean Tech Opportunities Management, Green Building Opportunities Management, and Renewable Energy Opportunities Management key issue scores from MSCI ESG Ratings. Missing key issues substituted with zero.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix III: Climate Risk Management Weighted Average Score
definition_notes: If weighted average is zero or uncalculable AND carbon emissions key issue weight is zero, score is set equal to Carbon Emissions Management Score
Key issue weights come from MSCI ESG Ratings methodology

mock_datapoints.name: carbon_emissions_mgmt_score
mock_datapoints.description: Carbon Emissions Management key issue management score from MSCI ESG Ratings; used as fallback for CRM Weighted Average Score when weighted average is zero and carbon emissions key issue weight is zero.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix III: Climate Risk Management Weighted Average Score

mock_datapoints.name: carbon_emissions_key_issue_weight
mock_datapoints.description: Key issue weight for Carbon Emissions Management in MSCI ESG Ratings; determines when the CRM score fallback to Carbon Emissions Management Score applies.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix III: Climate Risk Management Weighted Average Score

mock_datapoints.name: green_business_revenue_pct
mock_datapoints.description: Cumulative percentage revenue from six Clean Tech themes: Alternative Energy, Energy Efficiency, Sustainable Water, Green Building, Pollution Prevention, Sustainable Agriculture (sourced from MSCI Sustainable Impact Metrics).
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix IV: Calculation of Green Business Revenue

mock_datapoints.name: emission_track_record
mock_datapoints.description: Geometric average of last 3 yearly year-on-year emission changes. Assigned only to securities where: Average Yearly Emission Change < -2%, security reports Scope 1 and 2 emissions, and security has published an emission reduction target. Null for securities not meeting all three conditions or with fewer than 4 years of data.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix II: Calculation of Security Level Scores
definition_notes: Since a favorable Track Record is negative, lower values of the Track Record indicate a stronger historic reduction in emissions

mock_datapoints.name: credible_track_record
mock_datapoints.description: True if security's Track Record Score is 1.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix II: Calculation of Security Level Scores
mock_datapoints.semantic_threshold: Security is assessed to have Credible Track Record if it is assigned a Track Record Score of 1

mock_datapoints.name: track_record_score
mock_datapoints.description: Sector-relative quartile assigned from Emission Track Record; 1 indicates strongest historic emission reduction within sector; null for securities without an Emission Track Record value.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix II: Calculation of Security Level Scores

mock_datapoints.name: reports_scope_1_2_emissions
mock_datapoints.description: True if security reports both Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions; required condition for Emission Track Record assignment.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix II: Calculation of Security Level Scores

mock_datapoints.name: has_emission_reduction_target
mock_datapoints.description: True if security has published an emission reduction target; required condition for Emission Track Record assignment.
mock_datapoints.source_section: Appendix II: Calculation of Security Level Scores

mock_datapoints.name: gics_sector
mock_datapoints.distribution: choice(['Energy','Materials','Industrials','Consumer Discretionary','Consumer Staples','Health Care','Financials','Information Technology','Communication Services','Utilities','Real Estate'])
mock_datapoints.description: GICS sector classification used for all sector-relative quartile calculations and sector weight capping.
mock_datapoints.source_section: 2.3.2 Ranked Universe

mock_datapoints.name: ff_mcap
mock_datapoints.description: Float-adjusted market capitalization in USD; used as tie-breaker in sector ranking and as basis for Parent Index weight renormalization.
mock_datapoints.source_section: 2.3.2 Ranked Universe

mock_datapoints.name: is_current_constituent
mock_datapoints.description: True if security is an existing constituent of the index at the time of the review; determines incumbent preference in the 40-60% buffer band.
mock_datapoints.source_section: 2.3.3 Security Selection

mock_datapoints.name: parent_index_weight
mock_datapoints.description: Weight of the security in the Parent Index prior to renormalization; basis for both weight calculation (section 2.4) and active sector weight computation (section 2.4.1).
mock_datapoints.source_section: 2.4 Weighting Of Selected Securities

mock_datapoints.name: issuer_id
mock_datapoints.description: Issuer identifier used to aggregate weights across multiple securities of the same issuer for the 5% issuer weight cap.
mock_datapoints.source_section: 2.4 Weighting Of Selected Securities

mock_datapoints.name: is_new_parent_addition
mock_datapoints.description: True if the security was newly added to the Parent Index (IPO or other early inclusion) since the last Index Review.
mock_datapoints.source_section: 3.3 Ongoing Event-Related Changes

mock_datapoints.name: is_parent_deletion_event
mock_datapoints.description: True if the security is being deleted from the Parent Index at the event-implementation date.
mock_datapoints.source_section: 3.3 Ongoing Event-Related Changes

mock_datapoints.name: is_acquisition_target
mock_datapoints.description: True if the security is the target of an M&A event at the event-implementation date.
mock_datapoints.source_section: 3.3 Ongoing Event-Related Changes

mock_datapoints.name: acquirer_is_index_constituent
mock_datapoints.description: True if the acquirer in the M&A event is already an index constituent.
mock_datapoints.source_section: 3.3 Ongoing Event-Related Changes

mock_datapoints.name: has_characteristics_change
mock_datapoints.description: True if the security has a change in characteristics (country, sector, size segment, etc.) since the last Index Review.
mock_datapoints.source_section: 3.3 Ongoing Event-Related Changes

scoped_out.concern: Narrative description of index purpose; no transformation rules.

scoped_out.quote: 4. MSCI Solutions ... 4.1 MSCI ESG Ratings ... 4.2 MSCI Impact Solutions ... 4.3 MSCI Controversies ... 4.4 MSCI Business Involvement Screening Research ... 4.5 MSCI Climate Change Metrics ... 4.5.1 Fossil Fuels and Power Generation Metrics ... 4.5.2 Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Emissions
scoped_out.concern: Product descriptions for data providers; no index construction transformation rules. Data products captured in data_requirements.mandatory_products.

scoped_out.quote: Prior to the May 2020 Semi-Annual Index Review (SAIR) of the Indexes, the Green House Gas (GHG) Intensity has been calculated based on Scope 1+2 Emissions. The Scope 3 Emissions used in the May 2020 Semi-Annual Index Review of the Indexes is as of Jun 30, 2020
scoped_out.concern: Footnote 8: historical methodology variant prior to May 2020; not part of the current methodology.

scoped_out.quote: During the May 2022 Semi Annual Index review, securities are selected from the top until the number of selected stocks is less than or equal to the 50% of the number of securities in the Applicable Universe
scoped_out.concern: Footnote 12: one-time historical exception for the May 2022 SAIR; not part of the current methodology.

scoped_out.quote: Prior to October 31, 2019, each periodic rebalancing of the Index applied the 'Reporting Emission Reduction Targets' value as of October 31, 2019 and the 'Approved Science Based Targets' value as of November 30, 2021.
scoped_out.concern: Footnote 13: historical data-lineage note preceding the current methodology; no current transformation.

scoped_out.quote: Prior to June 1, 2020, security level GHG Emission = Emissions from Scope 1+2
scoped_out.concern: Footnote 14: historical formula prior to June 2020; superseded by Scope 1+2+3 definition currently in effect.

scoped_out.quote: Appendix V: MSCI Extended Climate Action Indexes
scoped_out.concern: Defines a separate variant index family (Extended Climate Action) with an additional civilian firearm producer exclusion and a monthly review cadence. This variant warrants its own methbook with base_methbook: climate_action listing only the delta rules. Not part of the base Climate Action methodology.
URL registry for the Methodology Set; no transformation rules. All referenced documents captured in external_references.

scoped_out.quote: Appendix VII: Changes to this Document

clarifications_needed.quote: Active sector weights which are greater than +5% are capped at +5% and active sector weights which are smaller than -5% are floored at -5%. Sector weights of remaining sectors are renormalized so that aggregate weight of all sectors sum to 100%.
clarifications_needed.concern: The document does not specify whether the sector cap/floor is applied iteratively (repeated until no sector violates the band) or in a single pass. The redistribution to remaining sectors may itself push other sectors beyond the band, making the iteration count material.

clarifications_needed.quote: Additionally, the max issuer weights are capped at 5%.
clarifications_needed.concern: The document does not specify whether the issuer cap is applied iteratively after redistribution or in a single pass, nor whether it interacts with the active sector weight cap (e.g., which cap is applied first).

clarifications_needed.quote: In general, MSCI uses MSCI Solutions data ... as of the end of the month preceding the Index Reviews.
clarifications_needed.concern: The 95th percentile thresholds for Emission Intensity and Total Potential Carbon Emissions (section 2.2.3) are computed from MSCI ACWI constituents. The document does not explicitly state whether these thresholds are recalculated at every semi-annual review or held constant between reviews.

clarifications_needed.quote: Securities in the top group receive a score of 4 and securities in the bottom group receive a score of 1 within each sector.
clarifications_needed.concern: The document does not specify how securities are assigned when they fall exactly on a quartile boundary (e.g., the 25th security in a 100-security sector). The tie-breaking rule (descending ff_mcap) is stated for the ranked universe but not explicitly confirmed as also applying within each quartile boundary assignment.
